TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kavalan, story: KavNr-ancestor_ngengi

S.FORM: sanu pa iku tu zana baqbaqi aimu sunis zau.
S.TRANSL: 我要跟你們這些小孩說我們祖先的故事。
I am going to tell you, children, a story about our ancestors.

S.FORM: masang baqbaqita na kebalan baqiku zini tagilanan.
S.TRANSL: 從前我們噶瑪蘭的祖先從宜蘭來。
Long time ago, our Kavalan ancestors came from Yi-lan.

S.FORM: sadamuan qaniau ta tagilanan nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們從宜蘭縣過來。
They (came from) the county Yi-lan.

S.FORM: yau ti tagilanan mRaytaRun tu zna qemuway tu zna ya baqbaqian.
S.TRANSL: 在宜蘭祖先開墾水田, 耕種水田。
‘In Yi-lan ancestors cultivated fields of rice and planted rice.

S.FORM: Runanay matiw Ribawut semalaw tu tu sapaqanan tu su tu sunisna baqbaqina mniz.
S.TRANSL: 男人去捕魚打獵好讓老人小孩都有東西吃。
Men went fishing and hunting for food for all children and elders.

S.FORM: nayaw waway na kebalan tagilanan.
S.TRANSL: 這就是噶瑪蘭人在宜蘭的生活方式。
And this is Kavalans’ way of life in Yi-lan.

S.FORM: tu masang mai tu lazan ta takalinku tangi nani suhuakonglu zinna nanganna lazan 'nay masang.
S.TRANSL: 從前花蓮都沒有路, 現在他們說蘇花公路, 就是那條路的名字。
And in the past, there were no roads in Hualien, and now they say Su-hua Road, the name of that road.

S.FORM: baq baqbaqi na kebalan baqbaqita ya semangi tu lazan sa sa kalinku.
S.TRANSL: 噶瑪蘭的祖先開拓通往花蓮的道路。
Our Kavalan ancestors broadened the road to Hualien.

S.FORM: nayau satuRin qenasianemanna baqbaqi na kebalan zaka.
S.TRANSL: 人們覺得噶瑪蘭的祖先很勇敢。
So they considered Kavalan ancestors brave.

S.FORM: tu nayau Rupun pa ti sayza nani yau ti lazan sa hualien.
S.TRANSL: 可能快要完工了, 然後就有了到花蓮的路。
Perhaps, (the road) was going to be finished, and then there was a road to Hualien.

S.FORM: masang yau tagilanan ya ya damu na baqian baibai baibaita.
S.TRANSL: 從前我們祖先的村莊是在宜蘭。
In the past, our ancestors’ village was in Yilan.

S.FORM: nani tu buRau ti pasa kalinku pasa e mataling sa pateRungan ya ya baqbaqiku.
S.TRANSL: 我的祖先搬往花蓮,然後又換到新社。
My ancestors moved towards Hualien, and then changed to Xin-she.

S.FORM: qemuway tu zna tapateRungan ya baqbaqita masang nani.
S.TRANSL: 從前我們的祖先在新社耕種水田。
In the past, our ancestors cultivated fields of rice in Xinshe.

S.FORM: Ribawut matiw smalau ya baqbaqi masang.
S.TRANSL: 從前祖先們去捕魚打獵。
In the past, (our) ancestors went fishing and hunting.

S.FORM: nayau qenalaluna na baqbaqita masang.
S.TRANSL: 我們從前的祖先是非常能幹的。
Our ancestors were very competent in the past.

S.FORM: ta tapateRungan ya saquduanna tu sunis 'nay sayza nani.
S.TRANSL: 也許他們在新社就是這樣養小孩的。
Perhaps (it was how) they nurtured (their) children in Xinshe.

S.FORM: yau ti ta ta si tapateRungan nazaw u lisin na saiza na na izau na baibaibudan.
S.TRANSL: 在新社我們的祖先有一些迷信。
There existed in Xinshe some superstitions of (our) ancestors.

S.FORM: m dada dalusa ti tasaw zinna nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們說已經半年了。
They said it’s been half a year.

S.FORM: temayta tu paRin tu tebaRiay 'nay napas zinta saRa samulay ti napas zinna nani.
S.TRANSL: 他們說「我們看到樹, Napas樹的紅葉,已經開花了」。
They said ‘we saw the tree, the red flowers of Napas tree. They’ve blossomed.’

S.FORM: smangi ti tu iza ya ya baqbaqian tu bawat saiza ipaz saiza na babaqian nani.
S.TRANSL: 老人們搭竹筏和船。
The elders took raft and boat.

S.FORM: nayau ti qibawutanna na pasaqutanna tu sunsunis mniz.
S.TRANSL: 那樣(赤桐花開花後),他們去捕魚,叫所有的小孩搬(竹筏&船)。
In that way, they caught fish and had all the children move (rafts and boats).

S.FORM: zuma nani baibudan e u saisi pa ita ti tangi.
S.TRANSL: 另外,女生老人要釀糯米酒了。
In addition, female elders were going to make wine from glutinous rice.

S.FORM: wa zusa pa ti tasaw zinna baibudan.
S.TRANSL: 現在要邁入第二年了, 老人這麼說。
‘Now, it is going to be the second year’, said the female elders.

S.FORM: tu saisi ti e sa e mulmuz ti tu nuzung saisi ti.
S.TRANSL: (我們)要釀糯米酒了,(所以)蒸糯米。
(We) were to make wine from glutinous rice, (so we) steamed glutinous rice.

S.FORM: temuzus ti ya tasaw qangid ti ita paskua zinna nani.
S.TRANSL: 過年了,我們快過年了, 他們說。
New year was coming soon and we were to have New Year, said they.

S.FORM: maqenu saisi ti a a tangananna ti tu isi baibaibudan masang saiza.
S.TRANSL: 老人們真的打開了糯米酒(的罐子)。
The female elder really opened (the can of) the wine made from glutinous rice.

S.FORM: palilin pa ita zinna maqenu.
S.TRANSL: 我們要祭祖,他們說。
‘We are going to worship our ancestors,’ they said.

S.FORM: mlilin ti baibudan masang nani nayau.
S.TRANSL: 女老人家當時就祭祖,那樣。
Female elders worshipped ancestors at that time.

S.FORM: izaanta na kebalan nayauna paskua aita kebalan ya ya mlilinay ya izau tu zana bab zana baibaibudan masang nayau lalasna nani.
S.TRANSL: 那樣的事情是我們婦女過年前做的事情。
These things were what female elder had to do before New Year.

S.FORM: yau a sunis a inging ti ita zinna sayza na na baqbaqi sunsunis nani.
S.TRANSL: 有小孩…我們都有空閒, 老人和小孩說。
There were children. …We had free time, said male elders and children.

S.FORM: u qatiw pa ita sa lazin zinna qaqaqaay nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們要去海邊,比較大的小孩說。
’We will go to the sea,’ said the older children.

S.FORM: tu maqenu siaungzus qatiw pa ita sa lazin pa pazing pa ita zinna tu matiw ti masusulun mai paqenanem mai kinausa Raw.
S.TRANSL: 一起去,不是單獨一個人,也不是兩個人。
Going together, not alone and not in two.

S.FORM: masusulun kintulu sayza kinsepat saiza kinlima tu wiya ti sa lazin.
S.TRANSL: 三個,四個,五個人一起去海邊。
Going to the sea together in three, four or five.

S.FORM: pazing tu bawut sayza qiqaiR sayza qiqabus sayza ya Runanay matiw sa lazin nani.
S.TRANSL: 男人到海邊射魚,抓九孔和龍蝦。
Men went to the sea to shoot fish, catch holiotis.diversicolor.supertexta and lobsters.

S.FORM: tutu ti mai mlawung tu nikuayanna.
S.TRANSL: 中午了,他們對於他們所收穫得並不吝嗇。
It’s noon and they were not stingy with what they’ve got.

S.FORM: temaqanna bawutna saiza.
S.TRANSL: 他們烤魚。
They grilled their fish.

S.FORM: nayau qabus Ramazanna qaiR Ramazanna.
S.TRANSL: 龍蝦,用煮的,九孔,用煮的。
Lobsters, they boiled; holiotis.diversicolor.supertexta, they boiled.

S.FORM: sakaputan Raw mai tu qa simqalawung mniz ya sakaputan ya sa lazin nayau wawai na Runanay.
S.TRANSL: 一起去的男人不對彼此吝嗇所有的東西。
The men going together were not stingy to each other with all the things.

S.FORM: matiw sa lazin mai mwazas tu tamun.
S.TRANSL: (他們)去海邊不帶菜。
(They) went to the sea without bring any meals.

S.FORM: wama saiza 'may siri' ya mian ya na na Runanay.
S.TRANSL: 只有飯,辣椒和鹽。
only rice, pepper, and salt.

S.FORM: aimi tazungan nani a qatiw pa ita sianguzus tina tina zinna tinaku masang nani maqenu.
S.TRANSL: 我們女生去撿海菜/貝類,我媽媽從前說。
We females went to pick food at the seaside, my mother said in the past.

S.FORM: aiya sianguzus pa ita Raw zinku tu temabu ti iku tu 'may mian siri'.
S.TRANSL: 就準備飯鹽和辣椒。
Prepare rice, salt and pepper.

S.FORM: niazasku tu matiw ti imi.
S.TRANSL: 這是我們帶去的東西。
These are the things we took to go (there).

S.FORM: masulun iku tu tinaku.
S.TRANSL: 我跟媽媽一起(去)。
I (went) with my mother.

S.FORM: qiala imi tu pais.
S.TRANSL: 我們撿海貝。
We picked up shells.

S.FORM: qiala imi tu tuqu.
S.TRANSL: 我們撿海螺。
We picked up conches.

S.FORM: qiala imi tu buqut.
S.TRANSL: 我們撿海貝。
We picked up shells.

S.FORM: qiala imi tu Rumung.
S.TRANSL: 我們撿海參。
We picked up sea cucumbers.

S.FORM: mala imi tu tu kapin qatutung niqaalaniq.
S.TRANSL: 我們撿紫菜和海菜類。
We picked up laver and other seaweed.

S.FORM: mwaza nikuayanniq.
S.TRANSL: 我們撿的東西很多。
What we picked up are many in number.

S.FORM: nayau aimi tazungan.
S.TRANSL: 我們女生就那樣。
That’s how we females are like.

S.FORM: tu sapatananan.
S.TRANSL: 我們要帶回家的東西。
what we would bring home

S.FORM: manan ti imu zinna bai talepawan nani.
S.TRANSL: 阿媽說,你們回來了。
‘You returned,’ said grandmother at home.

S.FORM: oh manan ti imi.
S.TRANSL: 我們回來了。
We’ve come back.

S.FORM: mwaza kenuayannumi.
S.TRANSL: 你們撿的東西多嗎?
Is what you got many in number?

S.FORM: mwaza bai zinniq nayau.
S.TRANSL: 我們說,阿媽,很多。
We said, ‘a lot, Grandma.’

S.FORM: masang yau pama baibainiq tapawan.
S.TRANSL: 阿媽還在家的時候。
(When) Grandmother were still at home,

S.FORM: sazaka aimi na kebalan Raw sanu pa iku.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭, 我要講說…
We Kavalan. I am going to talk about …

S.FORM: aita buguan sheme yuanzhuming aita nani.
S.TRANSL: 我們,不管什麼原住民,我們。
We…no matter what aborigines…we…

S.FORM: mwaza saiza sheme zu aita Raw a.
S.TRANSL: 很多族。
Many tribes

S.FORM: wama aita kebalan paskua yau palilin zinna.
S.TRANSL: 只有噶瑪蘭新年有palilin祭祖。
Only we Kavalan have palilin during New Year.

S.FORM: yau pa'tayay yau patuRqan zinta.
S.TRANSL: 有我們說的patuRqan招魂。
(We) have what we call patuRqan (conjuring spirits).

S.FORM: zana 'daqay mai tu patuRqan sayza.
S.TRANSL: 別人沒有招魂。
Other (tribes) do not have patuRqan.

S.FORM: a aimi kebalan aita na kebalan yau patuRqan paskua palilin Raw nayau.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭有招魂patuRqan,過年,祭祖palilin。
We Kavalan has patuRqan (conjuring spirits), New Year, and palilin (worshipping ancestors).

S.FORM: ya qazuma na na wawayta na kebalan zazaka.
S.TRANSL: 我們噶瑪蘭的習俗不同。
We Kavalan’s rituals are different.

S.FORM: timaisu sunis nayau ma sasanuanku zaka.
S.TRANSL: 你們小孩,我說的就這樣。
You children, that’s all what I wanted to say.
